features.name: Rage
features.description: In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action. While raging, you gain: advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, a bonus to melee damage rolls, and resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While you are not wearing any armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

features.name: Reckless Attack
features.description: When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn, but attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.

features.name: Danger Sense
features.description: You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells. To gain this benefit, you can't be blinded, deafened, or incapacitated.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

features.name: Fast Movement
features.description: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing heavy armor.

features.name: Feral Instinct
features.description: Your instincts are so honed that you have advantage on initiative rolls. Additionally, if you are surprised at the beginning of combat and aren't incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn, but only if you enter your rage before doing anything else.

features.name: Brutal Critical
features.description: You can roll one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack.

features.name: Relentless Rage
features.description: Your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 hit point instead.

features.name: Persistent Rage
features.description: Your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.

features.name: Indomitable Might
features.description: If your total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

features.name: Primal Champion
features.description: You embody the power of the wilds. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

features.name: Bardic Inspiration
features.description: You can inspire others through stirring words or music. To do so, you use a bonus action on your turn to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you who can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die, a d6.

features.name: Jack of All Trades
features.description: You can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

features.name: Song of Rest
features.description: You can use soothing music or oration to help revitalize your wounded allies during a short rest. If you or any friendly creatures who can hear your performance regain hit points at the end of the short rest by spending one or more Hit Dice, each of those creatures regains an extra 1d6 hit points.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: Choose two of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

features.name: Font of Inspiration
features.description: You regain all of your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short or long rest.

features.name: Countercharm
features.description: You gain the ability to use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects. As an action, you can start a performance that lasts until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Magical Secrets
features.description: You have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any classes, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast.

features.name: Superior Inspiration
features.description: When you roll initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use.

features.name: Channel Divinity
features.description: You gain the ability to channel divine energy directly from your deity, using that energy to fuel magical effects. You start with Turn Undead.

features.name: Turn Undead
features.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring the undead. Each undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw.

features.name: Destroy Undead
features.description: When an undead fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead feature, the creature is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is at or below a certain threshold.

features.name: Divine Intervention
features.description: You can call on your deity to intervene on your behalf when your need is great. Imploring your deity's aid requires you to use your action. Describe the assistance you seek, and roll percentile dice. If you roll a number equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes.

features.name: Fighting Style
features.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options: Archery, Defense, Dueling, Great Weapon Fighting, Protection, or Two-Weapon Fighting.

features.name: Second Wind
features.description: You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level.

features.name: Action Surge
features.description: You can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description: You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. The number of attacks increases to three when you reach 11th level and to four when you reach 20th level.

features.name: Indomitable
features.description: You can reroll a saving throw that you fail. If you do so, you must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: Choose two of your skill proficiencies, or one of your skill proficiencies and your proficiency with thieves' tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

features.name: Sneak Attack
features.description: You know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll.

features.name: Thieves' Cant
features.description: During your rogue training you learned thieves' cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that allows you to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation.

features.name: Cunning Action
features.description: Your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. You can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: When an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

features.name: Evasion
features.description: When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

features.name: Reliable Talent
features.description: You have refined your chosen skills until they approach perfection. Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

features.name: Blindsense
features.description: If you are able to hear, you are aware of the location of any hidden or invisible creature within 10 feet of you.

features.name: Slippery Mind
features.description: You have acquired greater mental strength. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.

features.name: Elusive
features.description: You are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.

features.name: Stroke of Luck
features.description: You have an uncanny knack for succeeding when you need to. If your attack misses a target within range, you can turn the miss into a hit. Alternatively, if you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description: You have learned to regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. Once per day when you finish a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up).

features.name: Spell Mastery
features.description: You have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a 1st-level wizard spell and a 2nd-level wizard spell that are in your spellbook. You can cast those spells at their lowest level without expending a spell slot when you have them prepared.

features.name: Signature Spells
features.description: You gain mastery over two powerful spells and can cast them with little effort. Choose two 3rd-level wizard spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, they don't count against the number of spells you have prepared, and you can cast each of them once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.description: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus.

traits.name: Lucky
traits.description: When you roll a 1 on the d20 for an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll.

traits.name: Brave
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being frightened.

traits.name: Halfling Nimbleness
traits.description: You can move through the space of any creature that is of a size larger than yours.

feats.name: Alert
feats.description: Always on the lookout for danger, you gain the following benefits:
benefits: You can't be surprised while you are conscious
Other creatures don't gain advantage on attack rolls against you as a result of being unseen by you

feats.name: Athlete
feats.description: You have undergone extensive physical training to gain the following benefits:
benefits: Standing up from prone uses only 5 feet of movement
Climbing doesn't cost you extra movement
Running long/high jump with only 5 feet movement

feats.name: Charger
feats.description: When you use your action to Dash, you can use a bonus action to make one melee weapon attack or to shove a creature. If you move at least 10 feet in a straight line immediately before taking this bonus action, you either gain a +5 bonus to the attack's damage roll or push the target up to 10 feet away from you.

feats.name: Great Weapon Master
feats.description: You've learned to put the weight of a weapon to your advantage. You gain the following benefits:
benefits: On critical hit or reducing creature to 0 HP with melee weapon, make one melee weapon attack as a bonus action
Before attacking with heavy weapon, can choose to take -5 to attack roll for +10 damage

feats.name: Lucky
feats.description: You have inexplicable luck. You have 3 luck points. Whenever you make an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, you can spend one luck point to roll an additional d20. You regain expended luck points when you finish a long rest.

feats.name: Sentinel
feats.description: You have mastered techniques to take advantage of every drop in any enemy's guard:
benefits: When you hit with opportunity attack, creature's speed becomes 0 for the rest of the turn
Creatures provoke opportunity attacks even if they take Disengage
When a creature within 5 feet attacks a target other than you, use reaction to make melee weapon attack against it

feats.name: Sharpshooter
benefits: Attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage
Ranged attacks ignore half cover and three-quarters cover
Before attacking with ranged weapon, can choose -5 to attack roll for +10 damage

feats.name: War Caster
feats.prerequisite: ability to cast at least one spell
feats.description: You have practiced casting spells in the midst of combat:
benefits: Advantage on Constitution saving throws to maintain concentration
Can perform somatic components even with weapons/shield in hands
When opportunity attack provoked, can cast a spell instead of melee attack
